response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine depth in developer tooling and testing infrastructure — test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD setup, Selenium training, and recent AI-agent tooling — which matches the domain focus well. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows neither: every position is an IC role, with leadership signals limited to teaching workshops and founding a small open-source project. The domain fit does not compensate for the missing core management requirement.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and test-infrastructure background (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI servers, Selenium course, MCP code generators)
18+ years of broad software development spanning web, mobile, desktop, and low-latency backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops, multi-day courses, and code katas, suggesting coaching aptitude
concerns: No people-management experience, against an explicit 2-year requirement for this manager role
No formal technical-leadership position; leadership evidence is side projects and teaching rather than owning team roadmaps or performance management
Much of the tooling work is personal open-source rather than build/test infrastructure at the scale of a large mobile platform ecosystem
